title: Oil Red O Staining
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"17mqr","text":"Adipogenic differentiation was induced by using the StemPro adipogenesis differentiation kit (Invitrogen, Carlsbad, CA, USA). WJCMSCs were grown in the adipose-inducing medium for 3 weeks. For Oil Red O staining, after induction, cells were fixed with 10% formalin for at least 1 h at room temperature. Next, cells were stained with the 60% Oil Red O in isopropanol as working solution for 10 min. The proportion of Oil Red O-positive cells was determined by counting stained cells under a light microscope. The final OD value in each group was normalized with the total protein concentrations prepared from a duplicate plate.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Cells were grown in the adipose-inducing medium for 2-3 weeks.
After induction, cells were fixed with 10% formalin for at least 1 h at room temperature.
Cells were stained with the 60% Oil Red O in isopropanol as working solution for 10 min.
The proportion of Oil Red O-positive cells was determined by counting stained cells under a light microscope.
The final OD value in each group was normalized with the total protein concentrations prepared from a duplicate plate.
creator: Yuejun Wang
